Items.Title: CSS Common Concepts
Items.Description:
S. M. Mahmudur Rahman

Posted on Mar 5

  1. What Flex layout? Difference Flex and grid layout?

Ans: Flex layout is a one-dimensional layout method for arranging items in rows or columns.
Flexbox is designed for one-dimensional layout whereas grid is 2 dimensional layout. Flexbox is designed for small scale layout while grid is for large scale layout.

  1. Explain CSS position property? What are some differences between absolute position and relative position?

Ans: CSS position property defines the position of an element in document. It works with top, bottom, left , right and Z-index to determine the final position of an element on a page. There are five different properties value which are static, relative, absolute, sticky, fixed.

Relative position places an element relative to it’s current position without changing the layout around it whereas Abosolute position places an element relative to its parent’s position with changing the layout around it.

  1. What is a box model? And what are the different elements of a box model?

Ans: Around every HTML element a rectangle box is wrapped. The box model consists of width and height of rectangle box and is used to determine width and height of the rectangle box.
There are main four elements in a box model which are content, padding, border, margin

  1. What is a Hover effect? What is the purpose of the active class?

Ans: CSS hover effect takes place when an user hover over the element and the element responds with transition effects. It is used to mark an element on web page and it is an effective way to improving user experience.

The use of :active pseudo-class to select an element which is activated when the user clicks on it.

  1. What are the different types of Selectors in CSS?

Ans: There are different types of Selectors in CSS which are listed below.

Universal Selector: The provided style will be applied to all elements by selecting all elements on a page

Element type Selector: The element selector selects HTML elements based on the element name.

ID Selector: The id selector uses the id attribute of an HTML element to select a specific element.

Class Selector: The class selector selects HTML elements with a specific class attribute.

Grouping Selector: The grouping selector selects all the HTML elements with the same style definitions.

Ans: It is a process of determining which CSS rule will be applied to an element.

7.** What is a CSS Preprocessor? What are some benefits of Sass?**

Ans: A CSS preprocessor is a scripting language that extends CSS and is compiled into regular CSS syntax.

There are some benefits of Sass which are given below. To write clean, easy and less CSS in a programming construct Contain fewer codes and help to write CSS quicker

Compatible with all version of CSS so that any available CSS libraries can be used.

More stable, powerful, and elegant because it is an extension of CSS. So, it is easy for designers and developers to work more efficiently and quickly.

  1. What is a Pseudo element? What is pseudo-class?

Ans: Pseudo Element: Task of Pseudo element create items that do not normally exist in the document tree. For example ::after, ::before, ::first-letter, ::first-line, ::selection

Pseudo Class: Pseudo class help us to select regular items under certain conditions. For example :hover, :focus, :link, :active, :visited

  1. How will you use media queries to make a website responsive?

Ans: There are many ways of media queries to make website responsive which are

  • Using min-width and max-width media queries
  • Multiple media queries for one call
  • Media queries for landscape layout
  • Media queries for portrait layout
  1. How will you make font size responsive?

Ans: Two best way to make font size responsive which is listed below:
By using vwunit for font size as a result font size will follow the size of browser window

Using media queries font size can be changed so that font size can vary on specific devices.

  1. What are the difference between reset CSS and normalze CSS?
    Ans: Normalize CSS means to make built-in browser styling whereas Reset CSS means to remove built-in browser styling

  2. How is the different border-box from content-box?

Ans: In content-box, there is height and the width properties consist only of the content by excluding the border and padding whereas border-box property includes the content, padding and border in the height and width properties

  1. Why should we use floating property in CSS?

Ans: For positioning HTML elements horizontally either towards the left or right of the container.

  1. What are the different ways to hide elements using CSS?

Ans: By using display:none, visibility:hidden, position:absolute

  1. What is cascading in CSS?

Ans: Cascading is a process of style declarations and defining weight or importance to the styling rules that help the browser to select what rules have to be applied in times of conflict.

  1. What is differences between nth-child() and nth-of-type?

Ans: The purpose of nth-child() pseudo-class is to match elements based on the number that represents the position of an element based on the siblings. The number is used to match an element on the basis of the element’s position amongst its siblings.

However, The nth-of-type() pseudo-class helps in matching the selector based on a number that represents the position of the element within the elements that are the siblings of its same type. The number can also be given as a function or give keywords like odd or even.

  1. What does ! important means in CSS?

Ans: Important will have the highest precedence and it overrides cascading property.

  1. What is the importance of CSS sprites?

Ans: CSS sprites is used for combining multiple images in a single larger image. Actually, it helps to represent icons that are used in the user interfaces. It reduces the number of HTTP requests to get data of multiple images.

  1. How does Z-index function?

Ans: While using CSS for positioning HTML elements, overlapping may occur. Z-index helps in specifying the overlapping element. The number of Z-index can be negative or positive and the default value is zero.

  1. What is the difference between padding and margin?

Ans: Padding creates space around the content of elements whereas margin creates space around elements. So we can say that margin generates space outer side of content of element but padding generates space inner side of content

Items.Title: Proposal writing for Google Summer of Code 2022 (4/5)
Items.Description:
Post (4/5) on Google Summer of Code (Long Post)

Let's talk about Proposal writing for Google Summer of Code

what is a proposal?
As per definition it is "a plan or suggestion, especially a formal or written one, put forward for consideration by others". If I relate this to GSoC then you have to mention all the small and large details of your project in a doc file.

There are two ways to write a proposal
1) With Template - some organisations provide a specific proposal template in which you have to fill in your information. For Ex- The Circuit Verse Org has its own Personal Template in which you have to fill out your Goals and milestones for the project.

2) Without Template - These proposals are made from scratch and they have to be according to applicant choice and with the help of suggestions from the mentors. let's discuss this in detail.

length of proposal form: This will depend on your project and the work you are going to do.

KEY HEADINGS

Title:
Short and precise

Your Info:
Your name along with basic information like your college, location, gender, email and link to your social media handles.

About:
Who you are, what do you do? explain in a short and simple form.

Synopsis:
Based on the format, start your proposal with a summary, designed to convince the reviewer to read the rest of the proposal

What will you bring to the Community?:
Ensure that your project aims to benefit the larger purpose by serving the community. Any reputed organization will aim to fund you if your proposal benefits a large audience with a significant impact.

Deliverables:
Include a brief, clear work breakdown structure with milestones and deadlines

Past Work:
Do your research, and make sure you understand how the project you are proposing fits into the target organisation

Biographical Information:
Be sure to communicate personal experiences and skills that might be relevant to the project. Summarise your education, work and open source experience. Any published work, successful open-source projects and the like should be mentioned.

Follow the rules:
Under all circumstances, you are requested to adhere to the guidelines that can help your application get a green signal.

GOLDEN TIPS

  • Be honest with what you write, unnecessary giving false information will not bring you good results

  • Take feedback from the community on your proposal form.

  • Plan things ahead of time and always have room for errors.
    ------------------------------------------------x------------------------------------------------

Give it a like if you made it so far Don't forget to read my other blogs

Bye :)

Items.Title: Angular global ngFor track by property directive with strict type checking.
Items.Description:
Nigro Simone

Posted on Mar 5

When displaying a list of data (at least somewhat large lists) you should be using Angular's trackBy feature which looks something like:

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; interface Item { id: number; name: string;
} @Component({ selector: 'app-root', template: ` <ul> <li *ngFor="let item of list; trackBy: trackById"> {{ item.id }} {{ item.name }} </li> </ul> `,
})
export class AppListComponent { public list: Array<Item> = [ { id: 0, name: 'foo' }, { id: 1, name: 'bar' }, { id: 2, name: 'baz' }, ]; public trackById(index: number, item: Item) { return item.id; }
}

Unfortunately, Angular forces us to write a tracking function in each component in which we want to make use of trackBy. With ng-for-track-by-property you could just handle this entirely in the template by passing a property like this:

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; interface Item { id: number; name: string;
} @Component({ selector: 'app-root', template: ` <ul> <li *ngFor="let item of list; trackByProperty: 'id'"> {{ item.id }} {{ item.name }} </li> </ul> `,
})
export class AppListComponent { public list: Array<Item> = [ { id: 0, name: 'foo' }, { id: 1, name: 'bar' }, { id: 2, name: 'baz' }, ];
}

ng-for-track-by-property has strict type checking and only available property are allowed

Get Started

Step 1: install ng-for-track-by-property

npm i ng-for-track-by-property

Step 2: Import NgForTrackByPropertyModule into your app module, eg.:

import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser';
import { NgModule } from '@angular/core';
import { AppComponent } from './app.component';
import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common';
import { NgForTrackByPropertyModule } from 'ng-for-track-by-property'; @NgModule({ declarations: [AppComponent], imports: [ BrowserModule, CommonModule, NgForTrackByPropertyModule, ], providers: [], bootstrap: [AppComponent], ],
})
export class AppModule { }

Step 3: add trackByProperty to your ngFor, eg.:

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; interface Item { id: number; name: string;
} @Component({ selector: 'app-root', template: ` <ul> <li *ngFor="let item of list; trackByProperty: 'id'"> {{ item.id }} {{ item.name }} </li> </ul> `,
})
export class AppComponent { public list: Array<Item> = [ { id: 0, name: 'foo' }, { id: 1, name: 'bar' }, { id: 2, name: 'baz' }, ];
}

you can also track by index with trackByIndex, eg.:

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; interface Item { id: number; name: string;
} @Component({ selector: 'app-root', template: ` <ul> <li *ngFor="let item of list; trackByIndex"> {{ item.id }} {{ item.name }} </li> </ul> `,
})
export class AppComponent { public list: Array<Item> = [ { id: 0, name: 'foo' }, { id: 1, name: 'bar' }, { id: 2, name: 'baz' }, ];
}

since track by property id is a very common case, there is also trackById:

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; interface Item { id: number; name: string;
} @Component({ selector: 'app-root', template: ` <ul> <li *ngFor="let item of list; trackById"> {{ item.id }} {{ item.name }} </li> </ul> `,
})
export class AppComponent { public list: Array<Item> = [ { id: 0, name: 'foo' }, { id: 1, name: 'bar' }, { id: 2, name: 'baz' }, ];
}

See the stackblitz demo.

Items.Title: MuleSoft Certified Platform Architect – Level 1
Items.Description:
We all know that Mulesoft is a cloud-based technology that connects applications and devices. At present, Mulesoft Certification is one of the most popular Courses. Here's the schedule to obtain the certification Mulesoft

I will do my best to give all the necessary information required to get a Mulesoft Certification.

This agenda includes guidelines for MCPA certification, Exam Material, Exam Topic by Weightage Course Recap Recording Exam Mock Exam link, as well as an attempt at the exam.

Guidelines for Certification
To be able to get a certification to become a certified Mulesoft Architect, You need to know about the structure of Mulesoft.

PREREQUISITES:- Mulesoft Developer Level certification is strongly recommended for Mulesoft Architect certification People with prior experience with Mulesoft are the best candidates to be certified MuleSoft Module as well as Topic Weightage

There are ten exam topics, as well as their average marks, percentages, as well as important modules are listed.

  1. Designing APIs, APIs Interaction Interactions-its average score is 8, The percentage of the score is 13.79, and its most important training modules total 4,6.
  2. Controlling Access to APIs-its average score of 7, and the percentage of its score is 12.07. The most important learning component is 5.
  3. Establishing Organisational and Platform Foundations-its average score is 6. while the percent of its score is 10.34. The fundamental knowledge modules comprise 2,3.
  4. Following API-Led Connectivity - its average score is 6. and the percent of the score is 10.34. Its fundamental teaching modules count 2,4,6.
  5. Governing APIs on the Anypoint Platform - its average score is 6. its percent of the score is 10.34. Its primary learning module is 5.
  6. Architecting Performance on Resilient APIs. The average rating is six, and while the percentage of its score is 10.34, Its fundamental education modules have a score of 7,9.
  7. Explaining Application Network Basics: Its average rating is 5. and the percent of the score is 8.62. The fundamental learning modules comprise 1,2.
  8. Delivering APIs - its average score of 5 points, and the percent of the score is 8.62. The fundamental education modules have a score of 7,9.
  9. Deploying Mule Applications to Cloudhub - its average score is five, and the percent of the score is 8.62. The essential learning module is 7.
  10. Monitoring as well as Analyzing the Application Networks - its average score is 4, and the percent of its score is 6.90. Its most essential learning component is 10.

Note: The 4,6,7,9 modules are important. Topics related to API-Led Connectivity and policies are simple to select. Monitoring Cloud hub architecture, Scaling, Performance are not high scores topics.
The most popular topics can be found here...

Below are a few subjects that are useful for you. C4E Success Criteria's Anti-Corruption Layer HTTPS and VPC Port numbers (8092) Asking Questions about Caching - Security Methods Storage of Objects Scenario-based question on VPC Systems API EDM, BCDM concepts API Questions related to policy - Study Rate Limiting thoroughly IPSec Tunnel & VPC Peering Concepts Integration and Unit Testing Questions about the design of Experience Process, Experience and System APIs Concerns about Shared Load Balancing and Dedicated Load Balancing Fine-grained and coarse-grained APIs How do you prepare to take the certification exam?

To pass the Preparation of the Certification exam for the exam, you have to study the Video for 2 hours, which covers the Mulesoft Certification Course.

The next step is to add Mulesoft Documentation. In this section, you will receive the manuals and slides for Preparation. Then comes the Course Preparation Session. In this session, you'll receive the quick Refresher Course. You will also be given a test exam to help you understand the exam.

Practice with mock exams...

The biggest issue every student faces in exams will be the exam format. Here are some mock tests. Experts designed these mock tests from their own experience. These exams can't be repeated with greater than 35 questions. The minimum score required is 90% to attempt The Certification. There are three Mock tests. Links to these mock exams are available.

The format to take the Mulesoft Level-1 test is a multiple-choice closed book, which is proctored online or in a test center. The length of the test is the 58-question test. The duration of the test will be 120 mins (2hours). The scoring for the test is 70 percent. There could be a few essay Questions and Answers.

Things to be aware of when taking the certification exam I'll suggest you follow some guidelines for taking the exam Choose low-hanging fruits The smaller the questions, it will take less time, and you'll have more time to write an essay.

Write longer questions down for later review. - If you don't want to spend the time, start with shorter ones first.

Examine all questions and answers at the end exam. Review all questions and answer at the end of the exam to determine the mistakes you made or if you let something go unnoticed If you fail to pass Review topics with low scores

Take the exam the next day

Important note: Tests conducted online by a Proctor Exam are offered You will be on your own when you are taking your exam Quiet and peaceful surroundings to prevent disturbances Exam administrators can request to see the environment for any ill-practices in order to prevent any

Exam administrators can halt the exam and cancel it when he observes suspicious activities

Final Concluding Point:
Overall, I would like to say that Mulesoft Architect Certification will open up great possibilities to be used in Cloud Computing Management in the future. I suggest you sign up with Cloud foundation and get your certification in Mulesoft Architect. Since Mulesoft is a software that connects applications and is a great option for the future, also, a well-known MuleSoft training Institute will guide you to get there.

Take a look at the Practice material and an explanation video for an understanding. Take everything into consideration. Take the Certification exam thoroughly and get become certified by Mulesoft. Do your best!!

I'm sure you find the Above Information covers most of the issues. If there's something that's not included in the above list, please let us know...

Items.Title: Quick Tip: Importing and Exporting Components in React App
Items.Description:
Mr. Paul Ishaili

Posted on Mar 5

In my course of Truly Understanding React, there have been a number of persisting issues which had not come to my full understanding until recently. Part of this issues includes understanding how component importing and exporting truly works in React.

Practically, skipping-that-part-to-understand when learning JavaScript contributes to this.

First, it is important to understand that their are two important ways of create a component in React App, and these include:

  • Creating component as a function, and
  • Creating component as a class

In the course of this article, including Truly Understanding React Series, the 'Creating Component as a Function' is being used.

Personally, it is has a result of my preference for functional and declarative programming paradigm (until I have discerned more).

In 'Creating component as a Function' there are different ways of doing so, as in the standard JavaScript syntax. The two most common ways are:

  • Using named function as in this case: 
function NewComponent (params) { 
   ...
}
  • Using Arrow function as in this case: 
const NewComponent = (params) => {
   ...
}

Exporting a component created using the Arrow function method to is very much the same as doing so with a component created the using named function method, but with a slight difference of limitation.

Exporting

To export a Component that is created with the named function method, there are four methods. 1.

export function NewComponent (params) { 
   ...
}

2.

function NewComponent (params) { 
   ...
}

export default NewComponent

3.

function NewComponent (params) { 
   ...
}

export NewComponent

4.

export default function NewComponent (params) { 
   ...
}

To export a Component that is created with the arrow function method, there are three methods.

1.

export const NewComponent = (params) => {
   ...
}

2.

const NewComponent = (params) => {
   ...
}

export default NewComponent

3.

const NewComponent = (params) => {
   ...
}

export NewComponent

Noticed the difference?

Yes, a component created with the arrow function cannot be exported instantly as default while creating it. It has to be declared after the component is created.

If you do know why, please, let us know in the comment section.

Importing

In importing the component into a new component file, it doesn't matter how the component was created (be it a function Component or a Class Componenet). What matters now is whether the component is exported as default, or not.

  • In the case of the component been exported as default, when importing, we use: 
import NewComponent from './newComponentFIle'
  • In the case of the component been exported not as default, when importing, we use: 
import { NewComponent } from './newComponentFIle'

Special thanks

Special thanks to all those who have found this series note-worthy to be reposted on other platform (and personally blogs).

I say thank you!

Public Notice:

If reposting on other sites and platforms, kindly give credits so everyone can be tagged and carried along in this series, thank you.

Stay Blessed, ✌ until next time.
Mr Paul Ishaili C.

Resources

Exports: MDN Web Docs. Retrieved from: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/web/javascript/reference/statements/export

*These React Fundamentals You Skip may be Killing You *: ohans Emmanuel. Retrieved from: https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/these-react-fundamentals-you-skip-may-be-killing-you-7629fb87dd4a/

Sebastian Bove Wagner - Feb 4

Items.Title: 5 Side Gig Options for Programmers & Developers
Items.Description:
You might be a programmer or a developer, but there’s no sense in boxing yourself into a specific category or title. The side gig economy is alive and well, meaning there are plenty of opportunities for you to earn supplemental income and diversify your professional skill set.

The question is, which opportunities are a good fit for you?

The Rise of the Side Gig

Sid gigs used to be seen as cute ways to make some extra beer money for the weekends. Today, it’s not uncommon for someone to earn a five- or six-figure income from side gigs. (Not so cute anymore, eh?)

The latest research shows 34 percent of Americans currently have a side hustle. And of the Americans who have one, 67 percent have started their side hustle within the past three years. Nearly one-third began in 2020, with the onset of the pandemic.

Nearly a quarter of Americans have plans to start a side hustle this year, including many programmers and developers. People are doing so for a variety of reasons, including:

  • Increased income at a time when wages are stagnant, but inflation is soaring.
  • More monthly income to pay off debt and/or save for retirement.
  • Opportunity for greater diversity and/or creative outlets outside of a primary job.

According to the aforementioned study, 33 percent of Americans with a side gig say they do it for the diversity of income, while 25 percent say they’re saving for a specific financial goal.

5 Side Gig Options

Looking for side gig opportunities that will help you earn some extra money as a programmer or developer? Here are several to consider:

1. Freelance Work

Have you ever thought about taking what you’re doing full-time as a programmer and adding a freelance component to your career? This is a natural transition that allows you to potentially earn a bit more doing the same things you’re doing for your full-time employer. All you need is a profile on a site like UpWork or Fiverr to get started.

2. Bounty Programs

Did you know that software failures affect some $1.7 trillion in assets each year? Because of this steep price tag, many organizations are being proactive in their approach to identify and address software bugs.

They do this by setting up bounty programs where individual programmers get paid to find hidden problems. Websites like HackerOne and Bugcrowd regularly have opportunities to make money doing this.

3. Online Instruction

There are a lot of online learning platforms that are looking for instructors to create specific courses for their students. You might be able to strike a deal teaching a live or on-demand course. This could earn you several hundred to several thousand dollars per month.

4. Real Estate

Do you want to shift your approach and try something totally different? Real estate is a great option for side gig money. Not only does it offer the potential for high returns, but it’s also quite flexible.

There are several different directions you can go with real estate side gigs. One is to earn your real estate license and become an agent. (You can pick up clients here and there.) Another is to invest in income-producing rental properties.

This requires a bit more capital upfront, but can provide semi-passive income on a recurring basis.

5. Information Products

If you have a creative side to you and are willing to move in the direction of becoming an entrepreneur (not just a programmer), you might look into the option of selling information products.

The term “information products” is really just a catch-all for monetizable content that teaches people something. Examples include books, premium video content, and online courses.

Currently, there’s a lot of demand for good programming content on YouTube. As the industry grows, people are looking for ways to learn programming and development without expensive courses and degrees. If you launch a free YouTube channel teaching the techniques you know, you could eventually monetize it with ads and other sources of revenue.

Which Side Gig is Right for You?

When searching for a side gig, think about opportunities that align with your skills, personality, and schedule. Sometimes a side hustle will perfectly overlap with your full-time job as a programmer or developer, while other times it’s a totally different niche or industry.

Find something that fits into your weekly schedule and try it out for a few months. If you don’t like it, you can always shift gears and move in another direction.

Items.Title: How to Deploy a Simple Redis caching app built with Next.js and TailwindCSS to Netlify in 5 minutes
Items.Description:
Netlify is a popular static site hosting serverless platform. It is a popular way to build, deploy, and scale modern web applications in a much more scalable and secure way.

Netlify helps developers to launch websites and campaigns in minutes with no fuss. Netlify is built primarily for JAMstack sites, which unify JavaScript and APIs to allow applications that are well suited for both developers and content editors.

Features of Netlify

  • It delivers out-of-the-box continuous integration.
  • The platform allows continuous deployment through its support for Git repository deployment.
  • It allows developers to focus on building and deploying apps by abstracting all the maintenance work away from the developers.
  • The platform provides free SSL, CDN, and continuous integration.
  • It has built-in DNS management & SSL certificates.

How Netlify works?

  1. The developer writes code and stores it in a version control repository (e.g. GitHub).
  2. When a new change is merged into the main branch of the repository, a webhook notifies Netlify to deploy a new site.
  3. Netlify pulls the latest version of the app from the repository and runs a build command to generate the static site files
  4. Netlify then uses Plugins and internal code to make adjustments to your site, pre-render all of your pages in static HTML and improves it further /
  5. Once the build process gets completed, Netlify takes the static assets and pushes them to its global CDN for fast delivery.

In this tutorial, you will see how to deploy a simple Redis caching app built with Next.js and TailwindCSS to Netlify in 5 minutes.

Table of Contents

  • Step 1. Setup a Free Redis Enterprise Cloud Account
  • Step 2. Install Netlify CLI
  • Step 3. Clone the GitHub repository
  • Step 4. Login to Netlify via CLI
  • Step 5. Configure Continuous Deployment
  • Step 6. Pushing the changes to GitHub
  • Step 7. Open the Netlify Admin URL
  • Step 8. Add Environment Variable
  • Step 9. Trigger the deployment
  • Step 10. Accessing the app

Step 1. Setup a free Redis Enterprise Cloud account

Visit https://developer.redis.com/create/rediscloud/ and create a free Redis Enterprise Cloud account. Enable the “RediSearch” module while you create the Redis Enterprise Cloud database. Once you complete the tutorial, you will be provided with the database Endpoint URL and password. Save it for future reference.

For a limited time, use TIGER200 to get $200 credits on Redis Enterprise Cloud and try all the advanced capabilities!

🎉 Click here to sign up

Step 2. Install Netlify CLI

Netlify’s command line interface (CLI) lets you configure continuous deployment directly from the command line. Run the below command to install Netlify CLI on your local laptop:


npm install netlify-cli -g

Verify if Netlify is installed or not by running the below command:

 netlify version
 netlify-cli/8.15.3 darwin-x64 node-v14.17.3

Step 3. Clone the repository 

 git clone https://github.com/redis-developer/nextjs-redis-netlify

Step 4. Login to Netlify via CLI

To authenticate and obtain an access token using the command line, run the following command to login to your Netlify account:

This will open a browser window, asking you to log in with Netlify and grant access to Netlify CLI.
Once you authenticate, it will ask you to close the window and display the below results:

Result: 

  Already logged in via netlify config on your machine

  Run netlify status for account details

  To see all available commands run: netlify help

Step 5. Configure continuous deployment

The

netlify init

command allows you to configure continuous deployment for a new or existing site. It will also allow you to create netlify.toml file if it doesn't exists.

Items.Title: When and Why to migrate from spreadsheet to a Database
Items.Description:
As data becomes a focal parameter to consider before developing your solution. One must ask questions like

  1. When and why do we use a spreadsheet
  2. When and why do we shift to a database over a spreadsheet.

This blog will help you answer these questions.

Q. What is data?

Data is raw information. For example, your daily consumption of coffee. It is raw information about the amount of coffee you have consumed, but if you analyse it and gain insights from it. A few example would be,

  1. Types of coffee beans or coffee flavour
  2. How much sugar do you put into the coffee

Now that we have differentiated between information and data.

There are many formats to store and transfer data, these formats depend on the type of data. For example, one might write coffee ingredients directly on a piece of paper i.e unstructured or write it in a .csv file i.e structured or a combination of both i.e semi-structured.

When to use a spreadsheet and when to shift?

So when deciding if a spreadsheet still makes sense, the first thing to talk about is the structure of the data. Are we dealing with a simple list? Are there nested values? So we use spreadsheets for flat data or structured or data represented using rows and columns.

Another parameter is the size of the data.

Imagine having to pass that giant spreadsheet to your colleagues and multiple people are gonna have to be working on it, maybe at the same time, then you start to have a lot more trouble. The set of following questions are going to arise

  1. Who is going to own the data?
  2. How do you make sure that there are not two edits going through at the same time? And maybe you have to merge them at the end or account for discrepancies between multiple copies.

Maybe your company works with a solution that supports multi-tenancy, a big shout out to Google Cloud or Office 365 here for supporting multi-tenancy. But managing conflicts, managing traceability of multiple people managing the data starts to grow out of control. When you introduce different permission sets to edit different fields or different sections of the spreadsheet, that's when, in my opinion, spreadsheets fall over the fastest.

When thinking about if a spreadsheet makes sense, you need to think of how the data is gonna be used. Spreadsheets are at their strongest when you can see a lot of columns all at once to get a good feel for the data.

When to shift away from spreadsheet

When multiple people in multiple systems are accessing and editing the same data, the integrity of the data becomes questionable. And if you're in a company that handles health care or financial information in particular, who added it and why is very important for when an auditor knocks on your door. So potentially understanding who is editing the data, what is that in the data, where did data come from, that is known as data integrity on a more complex level that's known as data lineage.

Databases vary much than that spreadsheets enable you to monitor, trace, control, and manage your data quality and integrity, especially because many of the databases come with built-in audit logs on a per-field basis. Typically what a database supports are complex reporting requirements.

Whereas a spreadsheet is fantastic at simple one-off reports or maybe manually updated, databases are when you need data that is updated nightly, hourly, secondly, or maybe it is updated by multiple processes owned by many people.

But just so you know, and to reiterate, the rule of thumb is if you need more complex reporting, go with the database over a spreadsheet. To learn more about Databases and storage, refer to one of my previous blogs:

Fitness-based swimming can have a greater impact on the depreciation of swimsuits than swimming to enjoy. If you're a professional swimmer, you'll be swimming for several hours throughout the year. An average swimmer however may visit the pool just once or twice a week. The swimsuits worn by recreational swimmers who live in warmer climates will be more prone to decay than the ones used by people in regions that have seasonally warm temperatures. This is likely because these swimmers spend the whole year in the water, rather than just four or five months. It is essential to have a swimming suit that's both durable and cost-effective if you're serious swimmer. Cleaning Instructions for a Swimsuit • Wash after every use Without a reference, determining how often you should wash your bathing suit can be a challenge. Do you need to wash it once each week? Maybe once a month? Each time you swim? Even if you did not go into the water, cleaning your swimwear Australia every time you wear is the best way to ensure you are clean. You may be wondering why I should clean my swimsuit every so often. Wouldn't this be detrimental to it? Actually washing your swimsuit following every use can help prevent it from becoming discolored or damaged. The reason is that your bathing suit could come into contact with various chemicals and agents every when you put it on. Sunscreen chlorine, deodorant, salt water tanning oil, body and lotion are regular things that could cause damage and discoloration to your swimming suit. The best solution is not to stop wearing sunscreen or put yourself at risk of damaging your skin, but rather to take the necessary precautions to lower the risk of damage to your swimsuit's fabric or discoloration. The most effective method for doing this is to clean your swimwear every time you wear it to clean any oils, chemicals and other substances from the fabric before they can cause harm, stain or fade it. • Choose the Suitable Detergent When it comes time to wash your swimwear, selecting the correct detergent could have an enormous difference. It's important to not make use of the same detergent you use for your other clothes because it's too harsh on the material of your swimwear Australia . Instead, you should use the gentle detergent that is designed for fabrics that are delicate or have the highest amount of spandex. A mild detergent specially designed for swimming suits will remove contaminants and oils like chlorine from the suit's fine fabrics without creating damage. In addition, products designed especially for swimsuits typically contain substances which neutralize the toxins in the water of the pool, making sure that the fabric of the swimsuit isn't damaged. • When washing your clothes, take the sand off Have you ever come home from a beach trip to find the sand in every nook and crevice of your favorite swimsuit? If you're spotting the bikini with sand on it, make sure you shake it in the open air first. The majority of the sandy particles that are stuck on the garment can be eliminated by shaking the swimsuit vigorously. After you've given the swimsuit an adequate shake, turn the garment inside out and shake it once more. Now is the time to clean the dishes. Clean your suit for 10 seconds in water that is cold. Clean any areas of the fabric that have the sand might be visible. Lay the swimsuit on its side to dry after giving it a thorough washing (somewhere far in a shaded area). If your swimwear is completely dried, give it a second shake outside to get rid of any sand that remains. If you find any remaining sand, use an air-cool setting on the blow-dryer to blast it away. • Washing machine shouldn't be employed The washing machine could seem like the best place to clean your swimwear after each use, however, you shouldn't wash swimwear Australia within the washer. The movements and agitation are rough on the fragile fibers in your swimsuit fabric, causing them stretch, strain and fall apart. Your swimwear could end up damaged and in poor shape as a result of this. If you must clean your swimsuit, don't do it washing it in the washer instead wash it manually. • Hand Wash Only In the case of hand washing, there's an approach to ensure that your swimsuit is free of dirt and damage. For a proper hand washing of your clothes take a bath or sink with half full of cold water. Stir in one capful of mild detergent until it is completely dissolving. If needed white vinegar may be substituted for light detergent or soap. Allow your swimwear to stay inside the soapy solution 30 minutes after having successfully created the cleaning solution. Take your swimsuit out of the solution of washing after 30 minutes and wash it using cold water, ensuring that all the detergent is gone. • Don't Use The Dryer It's tempting to put the wet swimwear Australia into the dryer to dry it quicker however speed doesn't mean it's always better. The dryer could ruin your swimsuit because it agitates the delicate fibers and causing it to be damaged. Furthermore, the high temperature can cause the fabric to lose its ability to stretch. Instead, lay your swimsuit flat to dry. • In order to dry the swimming suit lay it on its side Drying your swimsuit on a rack, or a doorknob might seem like an efficient solution, but it could actually harm and stress your swimsuit, which can compromise the fit. If a swimsuit is dried and hanging and the water is still dripping in near the base of the suit and can stretch the fabric. Additionally, drying it over something or hanging it up can result in the swimsuit losing its shape. To ensure that your swimwear is in great shape lay it flat to dry. • Do not expose yourself to direct sun for drying A common mistake that people make when washing swimwear Australia is washing them, then hang them out to dry in the sun. Although this could speed up drying however, it causes the fabric's color to fade faster. When sunlight interacts with wet fabric for swimming, the moist fabric is more prone to fade than when sunlight is interacting with dry fabric. After washing your swimsuit, do not dry your swimsuit in sunlight, as it will fade the color. Visit our site: https://www.novaswimwear.com/

Maybe once a month? Every time you swim? Even if you did not go into the water, cleaning your swimwear Australia every time you wear is the most effective practice. You may be wondering why I should clean my swimsuit every so often. Doesn't it cause harm to it? Actually washing your swimsuit after every use can help prevent it from getting discolored or damaged. The reason is because your swimsuit may come in contact with various chemicals and agents every whenever you use it. Sunscreen chlorine, deodorant salt water body and tanning oils and lotion are regular things that could cause damage and discoloration to your swimming suit. The best solution is not to quit wearing sunscreen and the risk of damaging your skin but rather to take the necessary steps to minimize the chance of damage to your swimsuit's fabric or discoloration. The easiest way to do this is to clean your swimwear every time you wear it to clean any chemicals, oils, and other products from your fabric prior to them causing cause harm, stain or cause discoloration. • Choose the Suitable Detergent When it comes time to wash your swimwear, selecting the correct detergent could have an enormous difference. It is important not to apply the same detergent that you use for your other laundry since it's too harsh on the material of your swimwear Australia . Instead, choose an appropriate detergent designed for fabrics that are delicate or have the highest amount of spandex. A mild detergent specially designed for swimming suits will remove contaminants and oils like chlorine from the suit's fine materials, without damaging the fabric. Additionally, the detergents specifically designed for swimsuits typically contain substances that neutralize the harmful substances found in water pools, ensuring that the fabric of the swimsuit isn't damaged. • When washing your clothes, remove the sand Have you ever come back from the beach only to find sandy sand in every corner and crevice of your favorite swimsuit? The majority of the sandy particles that are stuck to your swimsuit can be eliminated by vigorously shaking your swimsuit. After giving your swimsuit an adequate shake, turn it over and shake it once more. It's time to wash the dishes. Clean your suit for 10 seconds in water that is cold. Clean any areas of the fabric where there is sand. Lay the swimsuit flat to dry once you've given it a thorough washing (somewhere far in a shaded area). If your swimsuit has fully dried, give it a good shake to eliminate any remaining sand. If you find any sand left, you can use the cool setting of the blow-dryer to blast it away. • Washing machine shouldn't be employed The washing machine could seem like the ideal place to clean your swimwear after every use, but you shouldn't wash swimwear Australia inside the washer. The machine's motion and agitation are too rough to the fine fibers that make up the swimsuit fabric, causing them stretch, strain and fall apart. The swimsuit could become damaged and unfitting because of this. If you must wash your swimsuit, do not using the machine to wash it instead wash it manually? • Hand Wash Only If you are looking for a method to do hand washing, there's an approach to ensure that your swimsuit remains fresh and unaffected. To wash your clothes take a bath or sink half way with cold water. Mix in one capful of mild detergent until it is completely dissolving. If you need to white vinegar can be substituted for light detergent or soap. Allow your swimwear to stay within the solution of soap for 30 minutes after having successfully created the cleaning solution. Take your swimsuit out of the solution of washing after 30 minutes, and then rinse it off using cold water, ensuring that all the detergent is gone. • Don't Use The Dryer It's tempting to put the wet swimwear Australia into the dryer to speed up drying however, speed doesn't mean it's always better. The dryer could cause damage to your swimsuit due to the agitation of the delicate fibers and causing it to be damaged. Furthermore, the high temperature can cause the fabric to lose its flexible. Instead, lay your swimwear flat and dry. • For drying your swimming suit lay it on its side Hanging your swimsuit to dry on a rack, or a doorknob might seem like an ideal solution, however it could actually harm and stress your swimsuit, which can compromise the fit. When a swimsuit is dried and hanging and the water is still dripping in lower levels of suit stretching the fibers. Also, drying it over something or hanging it could result in the swimsuit losing its shape. To ensure that your swimwear is in great shape lay it flat and let it dry. • Beware of direct light for drying A common error common to people washing swimwear Australia is washing the swimsuits and then hang them out to dry in the sun. Although this could reduce drying time however, it makes the color of the swimsuit to fade faster. When sunlight interacts with wet swimsuit fabrics, the wet fabric is more prone to fade than when sunlight is interacting with dry fabric. After washing your swimsuit, make sure you don't dry your swimsuit in sunlight to preserve the color. Visit our site: https://www.novaswimwear.com/

Items.Description:
novaswimwear0003

Posted on Mar 5

Competitive swimming is a high-adrenaline sport that necessitates exceptional athleticism. It's critical to put on the proper gear in order to perform at your best. With our competition swimwear , you'll be able to show off your swimming skills in comfort and style. Our diversified customers can choose from a number of fabrics, sizes, and shapes, allowing them to browse a variety of racing swimsuits. Our competition swimwear is as diverse as it is inclusive. Regardless of your body type, height, or features, our extensive assortment is guaranteed to have something that piques your curiosity. You may be confident that when you wear one of our suits, you will look and feel your best. The best part is that you don't have to be concerned about breaking the rule. Look no farther than our competition swimwear for affordable selections with style and quality. Raw Materials used in Competition Swimwear Fabric is the main component. Some businesses make their own cloth, while others buy it from outside vendors. The fabric is dyed using synthetic colors. Dyes were extracted from animal, vegetable, and mineral sources until the mid-nineteenth century. While attempting to synthesis quinine in 1856, a young chemist in London named William H. Perkin discovered how to create mauveine, a purple color. Depending on the dye, the fabric, and the desired effect, dyes are applied in a variety of ways. The simplest method involves dipping cloth into a solution of water and dye. To make the color more uniform, an oxygen reagent is sometimes added. A moist metallic solution of tin, chromium, iron, or aluminum is applied directly to the fabric in mordant dying. The color is generated within the material when a dye is placed on top of it. Some dyes can be directly applied to the fabric. The fabric is submerged in a hot dye solution in this method. Fabrics woven with different types of yarn, such as nylon and polyester, are dyed to produce color patterns. Different dyes cause the yarns to behave differently, or not at all. A pattern appears on the fabric as a result of this method. Metal or plastic fasteners can be seen on the bra of a bikini or two-piece woman's swimsuit. A drawstring is commonly found in the waistband of men's swim trunks. Straps, leg openings, and waistbands are all made of elastic in various lengths. Design

Design is an important part of the swimsuit production process. Designers pay close attention to what is being worn and what is new in fabric and colour, just as they do in any other part of the fashion industry. Designers of competition swimwear are also concerned with fabric comfort, colorfastness, and flexibility.

Designers create new styles using a combination of hand sketching and computer-aided design tools (CAD). Hand sketches are expanded to form paper patterns, and a sample is cut from a muslin-like material. The garment is then fitted on a mannequin and tweaked until the designer is satisfied with the final result. Colors and fabrics are selected, and a sample is created and worn by a human model. The designer changes the design once more. Designers working with CAD use a stylus to draw on a digitising pad attached to a computer. The image of the garment displays on the computer screen as the designer draws. On the computer screen, you can also choose colours and fabrics. The manufacturer creates the templates, which are then cut into pattern pieces. The Production Methodology Swimsuit production is mostly an automated and mechanical operation, with factory personnel operating the machinery and guiding the fabric on occasion. • Knitting machines feed spools of cotton and synthetic thread onto knitting machines, which weave the strands into fabric rolls. The rolls are fed into big agitator-equipped tanks. In the tanks, pre-measured amounts of bleach and color-dyes are released. The cloth is then placed in drying machines after it has been washed and dyed to the desired hue. The cloth is re-rolled and placed in storage until it is required. • Fabric bolts are delivered to spreading and measuring tables by workers. The cloth is pulled across the table and wrapped around an empty bolt on the other side, with the bolts secured to one end of the table. The worker pulls the cloth taut across the table by turning the empty bolt to take up the slack. An encoder is used by the worker to enter specified length measurements. The information is subsequently relayed to electronic blades that cut the cloth by the encoder.

• After cutting the entire bolt of cloth into lengths, the worker stacks them up to 6 inches high (15 cm). The stacks are then taken to the piece-cutting machine. Another worker is in charge of the computerized gear that cuts the competition swimwear parts from the fabric lengths. Pattern-marking can be done by hand before the fabric is cut in smaller mills. The pattern dimensions are input into a computer in larger organizations, which then communicates the information to the cutting gear. The amount of components depends on the swimsuit's style. A one-piece maillot for a lady is normally made up of two pieces. The brief would be divided into two sections, and the bra would be divided into four to six pieces. Cups for the bra and the one-top pieces are also cut. For the crotch and the bodice, lining panels are cut. Swim trunks for men are made up of two to four panels.


• At separate sewing stations, each piece is attached to the next. Individual seamstresses working at industrial sewing machines or computerized stitching machines run by workers, depending on the scale of the plant, sew the garments. The bra cup is sandwiched between the lining and the front bra panels in a bikini, and the three sections are stitched together. Each of the front panels is then sewn with a side panel. If straps are required, elastic is inserted between two strap sections of your competition swimwear and the three components are sewed together. The bra's front and side panels are then stitched with the straps. A metal or plastic hook is sewed or pressed onto a facing on the end of one side panel if hooks are used to close the back of the bra. On the other side panel, a loop is created by folding the end piece over and stitching it to the panel. Briefs are sewn together in comparable ways, whether for a woman's two-piece suit or for a man's swim trunks. The front panel or panels have lining sewed into them. The front panel(s) and the back panel(s) are connected (s). Elastic is put into waistbands and leg openings in various lengths. The facing is made by folding the outer cloth over and stitching it to the garment. • The finished garments are ironed and sewn with labels on the inside. The swimsuits are wrapped in plastic bags and stacked into cartons for distribution to stores. Quality Assurance Before being sold to the general public, swimsuits go through a series of testing in the manufacturing. Repeated washings in fresh, salt, and chlorinated water, as well as exposure to simulated sunlight, are used to test fabric and colour changes. Before and after washings, the suits are stretched and weighted to see if they keep their original shapes. Volunteer testers are also given sample suits, who report back to the companies on their comfort and wear ability. Buy best competition swimwear

When your child joins a swim team, it might be intimidating if you are unfamiliar with the sport. If you're lucky, the coach will provide you with a list of necessary gear. I've discovered a few tricks for buying swim team competition swimwear.

  1. Fashion is less important than fashion The trendy trunks or bikini you wore to the local pool or beach won't cut it for swim team practice. Swimming for 45-60 minutes on a daily basis necessitates the use of a swimsuit with special qualities. Competition swimwear are designed for serious swimmers who have a hard practice routine. Look for flat seams to prevent skin irritation that enable full covering without restricting movement.
  2. Durability and chlorine resistant For daily practice, girls require a one-piece swimsuit. Choose a nylon blend if you want a low-cost, comfy suit that stretches well. Nylon suits are light, don't absorb much moisture, and are comfortable to wear. Unfortunately, nylon does not withstand chlorine and will not last as long as a polyester suit. Make careful to rinse the chlorinated pool water out of your nylon competition swimwear and hang it out to dry after each use to extend its life. As your child progresses on the swim team, you may want to upgrade to the more durable polyester suits, which keep their colour and shape even after hours of training. Boys can wear a swim brief or a pair of jammers for a bit additional covering. Briefs are usually made of nylon mixes to provide a secure fit and enough stretch to allow you to move freely. Jammers are almost as tight, but they extend almost all the way down the leg. The waists of both models contain a drawstring to keep them in place during dives.
  3. Selecting the appropriate size Buy your first suit from a shop that knows how to fit them if you can. Items.Title: How to Deploy a Simple Redis caching app built with Next.js and TailwindCSS to Netlify in 5 minutes
Items.Description:
Netlify is a popular static site hosting serverless platform. It is a popular way to build, deploy, and scale modern web applications in a much more scalable and secure way.

Netlify helps developers to launch websites and campaigns in minutes with no fuss. Netlify is built primarily for JAMstack sites, which unify JavaScript and APIs to allow applications that are well suited for both developers and content editors.

Features of Netlify

  • It delivers out-of-the-box continuous integration.
  • The platform allows continuous deployment through its support for Git repository deployment.
  • It allows developers to focus on building and deploying apps by abstracting all the maintenance work away from the developers.
  • The platform provides free SSL, CDN, and continuous integration.
  • It has built-in DNS management & SSL certificates.

How Netlify works?

  1. The developer writes code and stores it in a version control repository (e.g. GitHub).
  2. When a new change is merged into the main branch of the repository, a webhook notifies Netlify to deploy a new site.
  3. Netlify pulls the latest version of the app from the repository and runs a build command to generate the static site files
  4. Netlify then uses Plugins and internal code to make adjustments to your site, pre-render all of your pages in static HTML and improves it further /
  5. Once the build process gets completed, Netlify takes the static assets and pushes them to its global CDN for fast delivery.

In this tutorial, you will see how to deploy a simple Redis caching app built with Next.js and TailwindCSS to Netlify in 5 minutes.

Table of Contents

  • Step 1. Setup a Free Redis Enterprise Cloud Account
  • Step 2. Install Netlify CLI
  • Step 3. Clone the GitHub repository
  • Step 4. Login to Netlify via CLI
  • Step 5. Configure Continuous Deployment
  • Step 6. Pushing the changes to GitHub
  • Step 7. Open the Netlify Admin URL
  • Step 8. Add Environment Variable
  • Step 9. Trigger the deployment
  • Step 10. Accessing the app

Step 1. Setup a free Redis Enterprise Cloud account

Visit https://developer.redis.com/create/rediscloud/ and create a free Redis Enterprise Cloud account. Enable the “RediSearch” module while you create the Redis Enterprise Cloud database. Once you complete the tutorial, you will be provided with the database Endpoint URL and password. Save it for future reference.

For a limited time, use TIGER200 to get $200 credits on Redis Enterprise Cloud and try all the advanced capabilities!

🎉 Click here to sign up

Step 2. Install Netlify CLI

Netlify’s command line interface (CLI) lets you configure continuous deployment directly from the command line. Run the below command to install Netlify CLI on your local laptop:


npm install netlify-cli -g

Verify if Netlify is installed or not by running the below command:

 netlify version
 netlify-cli/8.15.3 darwin-x64 node-v14.17.3

Step 3. Clone the repository 

 git clone https://github.com/redis-developer/nextjs-redis-netlify

Step 4. Login to Netlify via CLI

To authenticate and obtain an access token using the command line, run the following command to login to your Netlify account:

This will open a browser window, asking you to log in with Netlify and grant access to Netlify CLI.
Once you authenticate, it will ask you to close the window and display the below results:

Result:

  Already logged in via netlify config on your machine

  Run netlify status for account details

  To see all available commands run: netlify help

Step 5. Configure continuous deployent

The

netlify init

command allows you to configure continuous deployment for a new or existing site. It will also allow you to create netlify.toml file if it doesn't exists.

FAQ's How to Maintain the Durability of Your Chlorine Resistant Swimwear? It's hard to part with the plus size swimwear that's been your go-to beach companion for many years. It's expensive to buy a swimsuit and deciding on the right one can be difficult. The reason why swimwear is discarded is many reasons. They lose form, color and loosen up, and become shabby as time passes. The fabric of swimsuits that is Lycra are damaged by the harsh effects of chlorinated swimming pools as well as exposure to sunlight. The rubber strips that are weaved inside the material of Lycra swimsuits break down and crack, much like elastic bands left in the sun, which causes your swimsuit's form to diminish and shape and elasticity. Swimwear that is chlorine resistant in size is an excellent choice since the fabric isn't flexible and can last longer than the typical Lycra swimsuit.

Chlorine resistant swimwear on contrary, is elastic legs and arm openings. Therefore, the proper maintenance is essential to extend the lifespan that your swimming suit will last.

• Treat the fabric prior to the first use The most frequent reason for fabric degrading in swimsuits is chlorine. An easy exercise prior to getting into the swimsuit may help in the effective fight of chlorine. Put the new suit in water that contains just a few drops of vinegar white for about a minute before dropping it into pure cold water. The vinegar assists the fabric's color set correctly, and the fabric's fibers will become water-based which makes them less prone to absorbing harmful chlorine. • Wash After Every Use After use, the swimming suit is often overlooked. On vacation, many individuals do not pay attention to washing their swimsuits. A residue of chlorine, the sun and other factors such sunscreen lotions, moisturizing creams, and tannin sprays all can affect the appearance and color of a swimming suit. This is why it is imperative to clean the swimsuit promptly after each use. If bacteria and other harmful microorganisms are found in chlorine-resistant swimwear that is neglected for a long period of time, contaminated by all these dangerous things and become an ideal habitat for harmful bacteria and organisms. This is then an effect on the fabric and the skin. • Use the Proper Washing Method Hand washing is essential for all chlorine resistant swimwear. Make sure to clean your hands with cold water, and then wash with a gentle shampoo or soap. Regular swimmers may also take a bath after washing their swimsuits by adding a few drops of de-chlorinator. • Your Swimwear Should Be Air-Dried After thoroughly washing the chlorine-resistant swimwear it should be dried in shade. Do not expose the swimwear in the sun's rays or washing it too vigorously. It is better to put the suit on its side rather than hang it up, since the former could cause excessive stretching. When you return the swimsuit in the closet, make sure that it's completely dry. • Use Chlorine Resistant Fabrics

Polyester spandex, nylon and other types of textiles are utilized for making plus-size chlorine resistant swimwear within Australia. Regular swimmers should pick materials with a high percentage of polyester and a lack of Lycra or elastane as it will allow the swimsuit to last longer. Swimmers competing in competition should wear chlorine-resistant swimming attire.

Why Choose Chlorine Resistant? Our swimsuits are constructed of 100% polyester. They can be worn in warm and chlorinated waters. This chlorine resistant collection of swimwear is constructed to last. What else do other people have to say about chlorine-resistant swimming wear? From the time I made my order, until I received them within four days, everything ran smoothly. "Beautiful chlorine-resistant swimming wear. The swimsuit has plenty of support. It's really beautiful. Chlorine Resistant Swimwear Care Guide • Shower before wearing your swimsuit

We suggest showering prior to wearing your chlorine resistant swimwear when you can do it. Deodorants, grease, grime cosmetic and skin creams as well as sunscreen, makeup leave-in conditioners and other products will stick on your swimming suit. Although these substances generally wash away in the water but the chemicals contained in them could cause harm. Oils, grease, sunscreen and various other chemicals are to be avoided.

• Contact with rough surfaces is not recommended If fabric comes in contact with rough surfaces the fabric could be snagged or break. Surfaces that are rough, like decks, pool walls as well as lane ropes, start blocks and concrete benches must be avoided. That means you should not sit on the decks of the pool or start blocks LANE ROPES or drag yourself from to the side of the pool. When you cross over to the other end of the swimming pool don't slip your body on to the top of the lanes ropes, instead, go through the rope. Place yourself on a towel or the kickboard or the bell of the pool. If you would like to relax on the edge of the pool, deck, or even the beginning blocks. To get out of the pool, always use the stairs. • Rinse your swimsuit after every use When you've put on your swimsuit during the gym or race, be sure to remove it immediately and wash it with cool, fresh water prior to lay it on the floor to dry. While we all want to take the hot shower after a workout or race but this is actually the most dangerous thing you could do. Your swimsuit shouldn't be ironed, bleached, or dry-cleaned, and it shouldn't be dried out by wringing it.

Note: Those of you who wear racing swimwear must be aware of this advice. The swimsuits are made of thin fabric and are designed to speed, not endurance. After each race, take off your chlorine resistant swimwear and wash it and place it on a flat surface to dry between races. When you attend a swimming event, don't wear your race swimsuit for the entire day as it is likely to become damaged.

• Do not fold your towel after swimming

It's all we have to do, and the majority of us do it on a regular basis, but it's also one of the most hazardous things you might do, especially when you put the towel in your swimming bag or in your car to use during the day or evening. Don't keep your wet swimsuit wrapped in a towel since the moisture, darkness and heat may encourage bacteria to multiply, which can cause the fabric to lose its shape more quickly.

Have you ever noticed your swimsuit is losing shape and turning green or yellowish color? This could be a sign of wear and burn from chlorine. You may have not experienced this in your swimwear Australia previously but others are already acquainted with the issue. The life span of a swimming suit is determined by a myriad of factors. The frequency you visit the pool, the amount of time you are in chlorinated waters, how often you do the same outfit (you must have a variety of options for your swimsuit) and, obviously, the suit's fabric are all important factors to be considered. The sport of fitness swimming is more detrimental to the value of your swimsuit than just for pleasure. If you're a professional swimmer, then you're likely to be at the pool for a number of hours each day of the year. An average swimmer however may visit the pool only once or twice per week. Swimming suits worn by leisure swimmers in warmer climates are likely to wear out faster than those that are worn by those in areas that have seasons of cold temperatures. This is probably because these swimmers spend the whole year in water, rather than just four or five months. It is essential to have a swimming suit which is durable and cost-effective if you're serious swimmer. It is also necessary to have at least two swimsuits which you could alternate to extend your life. In addition, you'll need another race-specific swimsuit you don't wear in practice! If you're an average swimmer (or an athlete who is who is casual) it's likely that you'll have plenty of swimsuits available to pick from to offer some trendy designs! A lot of trendy bikinis are available, even if they're not of the best quality. It's just a matter choosing the right one. Because swimming wear is expensive you need it to last for a long time. To get the best value for your money we suggest you purchase high-quality swimwear. Cleaning Instructions for a Swimsuit • Wash after every use Without a reference, determining the frequency of washing your bathing suit can be a challenge. Should you wash it only once each week? Maybe once a month? Every time you swim? Even if you did not go into the water, cleaning your swimwear Australia every time you wear is the best way to ensure you are clean. You may be wondering what the reason I clean my swimsuit every so often. Wouldn't this be detrimental to it? In reality washing your swimsuit after every use can help prevent it from becoming discolored or damaged. This is that your bathing suit could come into contact with various chemical agents every whenever you use it. Sunscreen chlorine, deodorant, salt water tanning oil, body and lotion are regular things that could cause damage and discoloration to your swimming suit. The answer isn't to stop wearing sunscreen or put yourself at risk of damaging your skin, but rather to take the necessary precautions to lower the risk of damage to the fabric of your swimsuit or discoloration. The most effective method for doing this is to clean your swimwear every time you wear it to get rid of any oils, chemicals and other products from your fabric before they can damage, stain, or fade it. • Choose the Suitable Detergent When it comes time to wash your swimwear, selecting the correct detergent could have an enormous difference. It's important to not apply the same detergent that you use for your other laundry since it's too harsh on fabrics of swimwear Australia Instead, choose the gentle detergent that is designed for fabrics that are delicate, or with the highest amount of spandex. A mild detergent specifically designed for swimming suits will remove the toxins and oils, such as chlorine from your bathing suit's fine fabrics without damaging the fabric. In addition, products designed especially for swimming suits often contain compounds that neutralize the harmful substances found in the water of the pool, making sure that the fabric of your swimsuit isn't damaged. • Prior to washing, remove the sand Have you ever come back from the beach only to find sandy sand in every corner and crevice of your favorite swimsuit? Don't succumb to the desire to dismiss it as an unimportant issue! Simple techniques can aid you in keeping your swimsuit from being tossed into the garbage bin stuffed with sand. If you're spotting the bikini with sand on it, make sure you shake it outdoors first. The majority of the sandy particles that are stuck to your swimsuit can be eliminated by vigorously shaking your swimsuit. After giving your swimsuit an adequate shake, turn it over and shake it up again. It's time to wash the dishes. Clean your suit for 10 hours in cold water. Clean any areas of the fabric that have there is sand. Lay the swimsuit flat to dry after giving the garment a thorough rinse (somewhere far of direct sun). If your swimwear is completely dried, give it a second shake to eliminate any sand that remains. If you find any sand left, you can use the cool setting of your blow dryer and blow it away. • Washing machine shouldn't be employed The washing machine might seem like the ideal place to clean your swimwear after each use, however, you shouldn't wash swimwear Australia inside the machine. The motion and agitation are too rough on the fragile fibers in your swimsuit fabric, which causes them to stretch, strain and fall apart. Your swimwear could end up damaged and in poor shape as a result of this. If you have to wash your swimsuit, do not using the machine to wash it and do it manually. • Hand Wash Only In the case of hand washing, there's an approach to ensure that your swimsuit remains fresh and unaffected. For a proper hand washing of your clothes take a bath or sink half way with cold water. Mix in one capful of mild detergent until it is completely dissolving. If needed white vinegar may be used in place of detergent or soap. Let your swimsuit remain within the solution of soap for 30 minutes after you've produced the cleaning solution. Take your swimsuit out of the soap solution after 30 minutes and wash it in cold water till the cleaner is gone. • Don't Use The Dryer It's tempting to put the wet swimwear Australia into the dryer to dry it quicker however, speed doesn't mean it's always better. Dryers can cause damage to your swimsuit due to the agitation of the delicate fibers which causes it to be damaged. Furthermore, the high temperature can cause the fabric to lose its ability to stretch. Instead, lay your swimwear flat and dry. • In order to dry the swimming suit lay it on its side Drying your swimsuit on a rack or doorknob could appear to be an ideal solution, however it could actually harm and wear down your swimsuit, and compromise the quality of your swimsuit. If a swimsuit is dried and hanging the remaining water will pool in near the base of the suit and can stretch the fabric. Also, drying it over something or hanging it could result in the swimsuit losing its shape. To ensure that your swimwear is in great shape lay it flat and let it dry. • Beware of direct sunlight for drying A common error that people make when washing swimwear Australia involves washing them, then hang them out to dry in the sun. Although this can reduce drying time however, it causes the fabric's color to fade faster. When sunlight is absorbed by moist swimsuit fabrics, the wet fabric will fade faster than when sunlight interacts with dry swimsuit fabrics. After washing your swimsuit, do not hang it out in the sun to preserve the color. Visit our site: https://www.novaswimwear.com/

Items.Title: What's a Rails Partial ?
Items.Description:
In a Rails Application, partial templates are a way of breaking down the view rendering processes into small bits. Partials allow us to reuse a block of code in many of others files and templates.

  • Partial are easily created by making a file _form.html.erb within the app/views/tags/_form.html.erb.

  • To use or render a Partial, we need to pass the render method within a view <%= render 'form' %>

  • We leave out the underscore when rendering the partial.

  • Important: To Render a partial in another file, we must use the path.

  • <%= render partial: 'form', locals: { tag: @tag } %>

Partial are a great way to practice the DRY philosophy.
Reference

The example below is an example of a form partial file.

<%= form_with(model: [@tag.bookmark, @tag]) do |f| %> <% if !@tag.bookmark %> <%= f.label :bookmark_id, "Bookmark:" %> <%= f.collection_select :bookmark_id, Bookmark.order(:name), :id, :name %> <% else %> <%= f.hidden_field :bookmark_id %> <% end %> <br><br> <%= f.label :name, 'Tag Name:' %> <%= f.text_field :name %><br><br> <%= f.submit %>
<% end %>
  • This Was Just a quick Overview of partials within a Ruby on Rails Application, Please check out the reference to the rails docs linked above the code block as this post only explain the basic use of form partials.
  • NOTE: This is one of 30 blogs that I would be attempting to write over the next 30 days to improve my writing skills.

Items.Title: All about Interfaces using OOP in Typescript
Items.Description:
When we are talking about OOP (Oriented Object Programming) one interesting and necessary topic is Interfaces. When to use? That's a design decision. How to manipulate? So, let's observe some cases and uses in Typescript language.

All the things declared in Interface has a public visibility and will be implemented into a class.

First of all, one example of its simple use:

interface IPerson {
   id: number,
   name: string,
   age: number
}

Perhaps, in some cases we can use Generics for typing a specific atribute in the implemented class:

interface IPerson<T> {
   id: T,
   name: string,
   age: number
}

One interface can extends other:

interface IPerson<T> {
   id: T,
   name: string,
   age: number
}

interface IUser extends IPerson<number> {
  password: string
}

We can use an Interface to typing an object:

interface IProduct {
   id: number,
   description: string,
}

let product: IProduct = {
  id: 1,
  description: "That's a computer"
}

Some attributes you can leave it optionally using "?" before ":", like this (age?:) below.

interface IPerson<T> {
   id: T,
   name: string,
   age?: number
}

It means that the Class will not not require it in a mandatory way. We can take methods too:

interface IPerson<T> {
   id: T,
   name: string,
   age: number,
   getId(): T
}

And let's implements the above example:

interface IPerson<T> {
   id: T,
   name: string,
   age: number,
   getId(): T
}

class Person implements IPerson<number> {
    id: number;
    name: string;
    age: number;

    constructor(){
        this.id = 0
        this.name = ""
        this.age = 0
    }

    getId(): number {
        return this.id
    }
}

If you need to do private our protected attributes, you need to adjust your syntax code, because the default visibility in interface is public. So, it's necessary to write the "_" before the attribute:

class Person implements Person<number> {
    private _id: number;
    private _name: string;
    private _age: number;

    constructor(){
        this._id = 0
        this._name = ""
        this._age = 0
    }

    set id(id: number){
        this._id = id
    }

    set name(name: string){
        this._name = name
    }

    set age(age: number){
        this._age = age
    }

    getId(): number {
        return this._id
    }
}

If you don't implement the set methods you get the error:

Class 'Person' incorrectly implements interface 'Person'. Type 'Person' is missing the following properties from type 'Person': id, name, age

One Class can implement multiple interfaces:

interface IPerson<T> {
   id: T,
   name: string,
   age: number,
   getId(): T
}

interface IUser {
    username: string,
    password: string
}

class Person implements IPerson<number>, IUser{
    id: number;
    name: string;
    age: number;
    username: string;
    password: string;

    constructor(){
        this.id = 0
        this.name = ""
        this.age = 0
        this.username = ""
        this.password = ""
    }

    getId(): number {
        return this.id
    }

}

A abstract class can implement multiple interfaces:

interface IPerson<T> {
   id: T,
   name: string,
   age: number,
   getId(): T
}

interface IUser {
    username: string,
    password: string
}

abstract class Person implements IPerson<number>, IUser{
    id: number;
    name: string;
    age: number;
    username: string;
    password: string;

    constructor(){
        this.id = 0
        this.name = ""
        this.age = 0
        this.username = ""
        this.password = ""
    }

    getId(): number {
        return this.id
    }

    abstract generatePassword():string

}

class User extends Person {
    generatePassword(): string {
        return Math.random().toFixed + "!@#$%¨&*()"
    }

}

For Type or Interface discussion see here.
https://dev.to/luizcalaca/how-to-use-in-typescript-type-or-interface-47jk

So, That's all folks!
